stem: The quadratic function g models the depth, in meters, below the surface of the water of a whale t minutes after the whale began a dive. The function estimates that the whale reached its maximum depth of 180 meters 5 minutes after it began the dive and then reached the surface of the water 10 minutes after it began the dive. What is the value of g(2)?
rationale: The vertex form is g(t) = a(t − 5)² + 180. Using g(10) = 0: 0 = 25a + 180, so a = −7.2. Then g(2) = −7.2(9) + 180 = −64.8 + 180 = 115.2. Hmm — but also g(0) should be 0 if starting from surface; the dive begins at surface, so g(0) = 0 and g(10) = 0. With both zeros, the function is g(t) = a(t)(t − 10) with maximum at t = 5: g(5) = a(5)(−5) = −25a = 180, so a = −7.2. Then g(2) = −7.2(2)(−8) = 115.2.
